title: McGill Financial Asset Management Hackathon
summary: QUARCC placed second at the Concordia Trading Cup, outperforming 12 competing teams in a day-long simulated trading competition.
detail:
Held in the John Molson School of Business, the Concordia Trading Cup tested teams on their ability to generate alpha in a realistic, instructor-curated simulated market environment over a full trading day.

QUARCC's strategy combined a momentum signal with a volatility-skew overlay to capitalize on earnings-related price dislocations. Despite a rough first hour, the team recovered to finish with a 12.4% return, narrowly edged out only on risk-adjusted metrics.

The competition featured live announcements of fake macro events, forcing teams to adapt their strategies in real time — a challenge QUARCC's members handled with poise.
